NKJV.1: Now as Jesus passed by, He saw a man who was blind from birth.
NKJV.2: And His disciples asked Him, saying, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
NKJV.3: Jesus answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned,  but that the works of God should be revealed in him.
NKJV.4: I must work the works of Him who sent Me while it is day; the night is coming when no one can work.
NKJV.5: As long as I am in the world,  I am the light of the world.”
NKJV.6: When He had said these things, He spat on the ground and made clay with the saliva; and He anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay.
NKJV.7: And He said to him, “Go, wash  in the pool of Siloam” (which is translated, Sent). So he went and washed, and came back seeing.
NKJV.8: Therefore the neighbors and those who previously had seen that he was blind said, “Is not this he who sat and begged?”
NKJV.9: Some said, “This is he.” Others said, “He is like him.” He said, “I am he.”
NKJV.10: Therefore they said to him, “How were your eyes opened?”
NKJV.11: He answered and said, “A Man called Jesus made clay and anointed my eyes and said to me, ‘Go to the pool of Siloam and wash.’ So I went and washed, and I received sight.”
NKJV.12: Then they said to him, “Where is He?” He said, “I do not know.”
NKJV.13: They brought him who formerly was blind to the Pharisees.
NKJV.14: Now it was a Sabbath when Jesus made the clay and opened his eyes.
NKJV.15: Then the Pharisees also asked him again how he had received his sight. He said to them, “He put clay on my eyes, and I washed, and I see.”
NKJV.16: Therefore some of the Pharisees said, “This Man is not from God, because He does not keep the Sabbath.” Others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” And there was a division among them.
NKJV.17: They said to the blind man again, “What do you say about Him because He opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”
NKJV.18: But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind and received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight.
NKJV.19: And they asked them, saying, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
NKJV.20: His parents answered them and said, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
NKJV.21: but by what means he now sees we do not know, or who opened his eyes we do not know. He is of age; ask him. He will speak for himself.”
NKJV.22: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had agreed already that if anyone confessed that He was Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.
NKJV.23: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
NKJV.24: So they again called the man who was blind, and said to him, “Give God the glory! We know that this Man is a sinner.”
NKJV.25: He answered and said, “Whether He is a sinner or not I do not know. One thing I know: that though I was blind, now I see.”
NKJV.26: Then they said to him again, “What did He do to you? How did He open your eyes?”
NKJV.27: He answered them, “I told you already, and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become His disciples?”
NKJV.28: Then they reviled him and said, “You are His disciple, but we are Moses’ disciples.
NKJV.29: We know that God spoke to Moses; as for this fellow, we do not know where He is from.”
NKJV.30: The man answered and said to them, “Why, this is a marvelous thing, that you do not know where He is from; yet He has opened my eyes!
NKJV.31: Now we know that God does not hear sinners; but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does His will, He hears him.
NKJV.32: Since the world began it has been unheard of that anyone opened the eyes of one who was born blind.
NKJV.33: If this Man were not from God, He could do nothing.”
NKJV.34: They answered and said to him, “You were completely born in sins, and are you teaching us?” And they cast him out.
NKJV.35: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when He had found him, He said to him, “Do you  believe in  the Son of God?”
NKJV.36: He answered and said, “Who is He, Lord, that I may believe in Him?”
NKJV.37: And Jesus said to him, “You have both seen Him and  it is He who is talking with you.”
NKJV.38: Then he said, “Lord, I believe!” And he worshiped Him.
NKJV.39: And Jesus said, “For judgment I have come into this world,  that those who do not see may see, and that those who see may be made blind.”
NKJV.40: Then some of the Pharisees who were with Him heard these words, and said to Him, “Are we blind also?”
NKJV.41: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, ‘We see.’ Therefore your sin remains.

ESV.1: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.
ESV.2: And his disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
ESV.3: Jesus answered, “It was not that this man sinned, or his parents, but that the works of God might be displayed in him.
ESV.4: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming, when no one can work.
ESV.5: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
ESV.6: Having said these things, he spit on the ground and made mud with the saliva. Then he anointed the man's eyes with the mud
ESV.7: and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). So he went and washed and came back seeing.
ESV.8: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar were saying, “Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?”
ESV.9: Some said, “It is he.” Others said, “No, but he is like him.” He kept saying, “I am the man.”
ESV.10: So they said to him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
ESV.11: He answered, “The man called Jesus made mud and anointed my eyes and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ So I went and washed and received my sight.”
ESV.12: They said to him, “Where is he?” He said, “I do not know.”
ESV.13: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
ESV.14: Now it was a Sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
ESV.15: So the Pharisees again asked him how he had received his sight. And he said to them, “He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and I see.”
ESV.16: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for he does not keep the Sabbath.” But others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” And there was a division among them.
ESV.17: So they said again to the blind man, “What do you say about him, since he has opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”
ESV.18: The Jews  did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight, until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight
ESV.19: and asked them, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
ESV.20: His parents answered, “We know that this is our son and that he was born blind.
ESV.21: But how he now sees we do not know, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself.”
ESV.22: (His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone should confess Jesus  to be Christ, he was to be put out of the synagogue.)
ESV.23: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
ESV.24: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.”
ESV.25: He answered, “Whether he is a sinner I do not know. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.”
ESV.26: They said to him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
ESV.27: He answered them, “I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
ESV.28: And they reviled him, saying, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
ESV.29: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from.”

NLT.1: As Jesus was walking along, he saw a man who had been blind from birth.
NLT.2: “Rabbi,” his disciples asked him, “why was this man born blind? Was it because of his own sins or his parents’ sins?”
NLT.3: “It was not because of his sins or his parents’ sins,” Jesus answered. “This happened so the power of God could be seen in him.
NLT.4: We must quickly carry out the tasks assigned us by the one who sent us. The night is coming, and then no one can work.
NLT.5: But while I am here in the world, I am the light of the world.”
NLT.6: Then he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and spread the mud over the blind man’s eyes.
NLT.7: He told him, “Go wash yourself in the pool of Siloam” (Siloam means “sent”). So the man went and washed and came back seeing!
NLT.8: His neighbors and others who knew him as a blind beggar asked each other, “Isn’t this the man who used to sit and beg?”
NLT.9: Some said he was, and others said, “No, he just looks like him!” But the beggar kept saying, “Yes, I am the same one!”
NLT.10: They asked, “Who healed you? What happened?”
NLT.11: He told them, “The man they call Jesus made mud and spread it over my eyes and told me, ‘Go to the pool of Siloam and wash yourself.’ So I went and washed, and now I can see!”
NLT.12: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don’t know,” he replied.
NLT.13: Then they took the man who had been blind to the Pharisees,
NLT.14: because it was on the Sabbath that Jesus had made the mud and healed him.
NLT.15: The Pharisees asked the man all about it. So he told them, “He put the mud over my eyes, and when I washed it away, I could see!”
NLT.16: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus is not from God, for he is working on the Sabbath.” Others said, “But how could an ordinary sinner do such miraculous signs?” So there was a deep division of opinion among them.
NLT.17: Then the Pharisees again questioned the man who had been blind and demanded, “What’s your opinion about this man who healed you?” The man replied, “I think he must be a prophet.”
NLT.18: The Jewish leaders still refused to believe the man had been blind and could now see, so they called in his parents.
NLT.19: They asked them, “Is this your son? Was he born blind? If so, how can he now see?”
NLT.20: His parents replied, “We know this is our son and that he was born blind,
NLT.21: but we don’t know how he can see or who healed him. Ask him. He is old enough to speak for himself.”
NLT.22: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders, who had announced that anyone saying Jesus was the Messiah would be expelled from the synagogue.
NLT.23: That’s why they said, “He is old enough. Ask him.”
NLT.24: So for the second time they called in the man who had been blind and told him, “God should get the glory for this, because we know this man Jesus is a sinner.”
NLT.25: “I don’t know whether he is a sinner,” the man replied. “But I know this: I was blind, and now I can see!”
NLT.26: “But what did he do?” they asked. “How did he heal you?”
NLT.27: “Look!” the man exclaimed. “I told you once. Didn’t you listen? Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples, too?”
NLT.28: Then they cursed him and said, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses!
NLT.29: We know God spoke to Moses, but we don’t even know where this man comes from.”
NLT.30: “Why, that’s very strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don’t know where he comes from?
NLT.31: We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but he is ready to hear those who worship him and do his will.
NLT.32: Ever since the world began, no one has been able to open the eyes of someone born blind.
NLT.33: If this man were not from God, he couldn’t have done it.”
NLT.34: “You were born a total sinner!” they answered. “Are you trying to teach us?” And they threw him out of the synagogue.
NLT.35: When Jesus heard what had happened, he found the man and asked, “Do you believe in the Son of Man? ”
NLT.36: The man answered, “Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.”
NLT.37: “You have seen him,” Jesus said, “and he is speaking to you!”
NLT.38: “Yes, Lord, I believe!” the man said. And he worshiped Jesus.
NLT.39: Then Jesus told him, “I entered this world to render judgment — to give sight to the blind and to show those who think they see that they are blind.”
NLT.40: Some Pharisees who were standing nearby heard him and asked, “Are you saying we’re blind?”
NLT.41: “If you were blind, you wouldn’t be guilty,” Jesus replied. “But you remain guilty because you claim you can see.

AMP.1: While He was passing by, He noticed a man [who had been] blind from birth.
AMP.2: His disciples asked Him, "Rabbi (Teacher), who sinned, this man or his parents, that he would be born blind?"
AMP.3: Jesus answered, "Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but it was so that the works of God might be displayed and illustrated in him.
AMP.4: We must work the works of Him who sent Me while it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
AMP.5: As long as I am in the world, I am the Light of the world [giving guidance through My word and works]."
AMP.6: When He had said this, He spat on the ground and made mud with His saliva, and He spread the mud [like an ointment] on the man's eyes.
AMP.7: And He said to him, "Go, wash in the pool of Siloam" (which is translated, Sent). So he went away and washed, and came back seeing.
AMP.8: So the neighbors, and those who used to know him as a beggar, said, "Is not this the man who used to sit and beg?"
AMP.9: Some said, "It is he." Still others said, "No, but he looks like him." But he kept saying, "I am the man."
AMP.10: So they said to him, "How were your eyes opened?"
AMP.11: He replied, "The Man called Jesus made mud and smeared it on my eyes and told me, 'Go to Siloam and wash.' So I went and washed, and I received my sight!"
AMP.12: They asked him, "Where is He?" He said, "I do not know."
AMP.13: Then they brought the man who was formerly blind to the Pharisees.
AMP.14: Now it was on a Sabbath day that Jesus made the mud and opened the man's eyes.
AMP.15: So the Pharisees asked him again how he received his sight. And he said to them, "He smeared mud on my eyes, and I washed, and now I see."
AMP.16: Then some of the Pharisees said, "This Man [Jesus] is not from God, because He does not keep the Sabbath." But others said, "How can a man who is a sinner (a non-observant Jew) do such signs and miracles?" So there was a difference of opinion among them.
AMP.17: Accordingly they said to the blind man again, "What do you say about Him, since He opened your eyes?" And he said, "[It must be that] He is a prophet!"
AMP.18: However, the Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they called the man's parents.
AMP.19: They asked them, "Is this your son, who you say was born blind? Then how does he now see?"
AMP.20: His parents answered, "We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
AMP.21: but as to how he now sees, we do not know; or who has opened his eyes, we do not know. Ask him [and stop asking us]; he is of age, he will speak for himself and give his own account of it."
AMP.22: His parents said this because they were afraid of [the leaders of] the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone acknowledged Jesus to be the Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue (excommunicated).
AMP.23: Because of this his parents said, "He is of age; ask him."
AMP.24: So a second time they called the man who had been [born] blind, and said to him, "Give God glory and praise [for your sight]! We know this Man [Jesus] is a sinner [separated from God]."
AMP.25: Then he answered, "I do not know whether He is a sinner [separated from God]; but one thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see."
AMP.26: So they said to him, "What did He [actually] do to you? How did He open your eyes?"
AMP.27: He answered, "I already told you and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again and again? Do you want to become His disciples, too?"
AMP.28: And [at that remark] they stormed at him and jeered, "You are His disciple, but we are disciples of Moses!
AMP.29: We know [for certain] that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this Man, we do not know where He is from."
AMP.30: The man replied, "Well, this is astonishing! You do not know where He comes from, and yet He opened my eyes!
AMP.31: We know [according to your tradition] that God does not hear sinners; but if anyone fears God and does His will, He hears him.
AMP.32: Since the beginning of time it has never been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a person born blind.
AMP.33: If this Man were not from God, He would not be able to do anything [like this because God would not hear His prayer]."
AMP.34: They answered him, "You were born entirely in sins [from head to foot], and you [presume to] teach us?" Then they threw him out [of the synagogue].
AMP.35: Jesus heard that they had put him out [of the synagogue], and finding him, He asked, "Do you believe in the Son of Man?"
AMP.36: He answered, "Who is He, Sir? Tell me so that I may believe in Him."
AMP.37: Jesus said to him, "You have both seen Him, and [in fact] He is the one who is talking with you."
AMP.38: And he said, "Lord, I believe [in You and Your word]!" And he worshiped Him [with reverence and awe].
AMP.39: Then Jesus said, "I came into this world for judgment [to separate those who believe in Me from those who reject Me—to declare judgment on those who choose to be separated from God], so that the sightless would see, and those who see would become blind."
AMP.40: Some Pharisees who were with Him heard these things and said to Him, "Are we also blind?"
AMP.41: Jesus said to them, "If you were blind [to spiritual things], you would have no sin [and would not be blamed for your unbelief]; but since you claim to have [spiritual] sight, [you have no excuse so] your sin and guilt remain.

NIV.1:
Jesus Heals a Man Born Blind
As he went along, he saw a man blind from birth.
NIV.2: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
NIV.3: “Neither this man nor his parents sinned,” said Jesus, “but this happened so that the work of God might be displayed in his life.
NIV.4: As long as it is day, we must do the work of him who sent me. Night is coming, when no one can work.
NIV.5: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
NIV.6: Having said this, he spit on the ground, made some mud with the saliva, and put it on the man’s eyes.
NIV.7: “Go,” he told him, “wash in the Pool of Siloam” (this word means Sent). So the man went and washed, and came home seeing.
NIV.8: His neighbors and those who had formerly seen him begging asked, “Isn’t this the same man who used to sit and beg?”
NIV.9:
Some claimed that he was.
Others said, “No, he only looks like him.”
But he himself insisted, “I am the man.”
NIV.10: “How then were your eyes opened?” they demanded.
NIV.11: He replied, “The man they call Jesus made some mud and put it on my eyes. He told me to go to Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.”
NIV.12:
“Where is this man?” they asked him.
“I don’t know,” he said.
NIV.13:
The Pharisees Investigate the Healing
They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.
NIV.14: Now the day on which Jesus had made the mud and opened the man’s eyes was a Sabbath.
NIV.15: Therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. “He put mud on my eyes,” the man replied, “and I washed, and now I see.”
NIV.16:
Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for he does not keep the Sabbath.”
But others asked, “How can a sinner do such miraculous signs?” So they were divided.
NIV.17:
Finally they turned again to the blind man, “What have you to say about him? It was your eyes he opened.”
The man replied, “He is a prophet.”
NIV.18: The Jews still did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they sent for the man’s parents.
NIV.19: “Is this your son?” they asked. “Is this the one you say was born blind? How is it that now he can see?”
NIV.20: “We know he is our son,” the parents answered, “and we know he was born blind.
NIV.21: But how he can see now, or who opened his eyes, we don’t know. Ask him. He is of age; he will speak for himself.”
NIV.22: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews, for already the Jews had decided that anyone who acknowledged that Jesus was the Christ would be put out of the synagogue.
NIV.23: That was why his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
NIV.24: A second time they summoned the man who had been blind. “Give glory to God,” they said. “We know this man is a sinner.”
NIV.25: He replied, “Whether he is a sinner or not, I don’t know. One thing I do know. I was blind but now I see!”
NIV.26: Then they asked him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
NIV.27: He answered, “I have told you already and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples, too?”
NIV.28: Then they hurled insults at him and said, “You are this fellow’s disciple! We are disciples of Moses!
NIV.29: We know that God spoke to Moses, but as for this fellow, we don’t even know where he comes from.”
NIV.30: The man answered, “Now that is remarkable! You don’t know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.
NIV.31: We know that God does not listen to sinners. He listens to the godly man who does his will.
NIV.32: Nobody has ever heard of opening the eyes of a man born blind.
NIV.33: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
NIV.34: To this they replied, “You were steeped in sin at birth; how dare you lecture us!” And they threw him out.
NIV.35:
Spiritual Blindness
Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and when he found him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
NIV.36: “Who is he, sir?” the man asked. “Tell me so that I may believe in him.”
NIV.37: Jesus said, “You have now seen him; in fact, he is the one speaking with you.”
NIV.38: Then the man said, “Lord, I believe,” and he worshiped him.
NIV.39: Jesus said, “For judgment I have come into this world, so that the blind will see and those who see will become blind.”
NIV.40: Some Pharisees who were with him heard him say this and asked, “What? Are we blind too?”
NIV.41: Jesus said, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty of sin; but now that you claim you can see, your guilt remains.

NET.1: Now as Jesus was passing by, he saw a man who had been blind from birth.

NET.4: We must perform the deeds of the one who sent me as long as it is daytime. Night is coming when no one can work.
